reasoning: The document's fidelity composite score of 0.8978 falls within the primary AUTO_APPROVE pathway (>= 0.85). Although there is a recorded omission in section PE-17 and heading hierarchy issues, the signal agreement is 'converge_with_minors' and all specific concerns are classified as minor severity. Per the routing instructions, a high composite score with only minor concerns necessitates automatic approval regardless of programmatic shingling values or pipeline routing.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: In section PE-17 (Alternate Work Site), the output document omits a significant portion of the requirements that appear at the top of Page 12 of the source. Specifically, nested list items d.ii through d.vi and top-level list item e are entirely absent. These items cover critical security requirements for secure data storage at alternate sites, equipment security at home/hotel sites, and vehicle transport rules. This omission represents more than 25% of the content for this specific section.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: ii. Design requirements for secure data storage (e.g., fire suppression and detection equipment, heating, ventilation, and air conditioning [HVAC], measures to prevent water damage, etc.). iii. Equipment being used or stored at an individual alternate work site, such as hotel, home, or other alternate site, must be secured when not in use. iv. Equipment transported in vehicles must be hidden from casual view. v. Equipment must not be stored in vehicles overnight. vi. NIST SP 800-46, Revision 2 m
concerns.output_quote: The list for PE-17 (element index 48) ends abruptly after item d.i: 'i. Sufficient physical access controls (e.g., doors, locks, etc.) to the site and to the agency’s data store.'

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is severely flattened and contains skips. The document title is rendered as an H3 (element index 1), jumping directly from the root level to H3 without H1 or H2. Furthermore, subsection headers like 'PE-6 (1)' (element index 31) and 'PE-13 (1)' (element index 42) are rendered as H3, making them peers to their parent section headers ('PE-6' at index 27 and 'PE-13' at index 40), which are also H3. This results in incorrect nesting and loss of semantic structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PE-6 (Page 6) is a section header; PE-6 (1) (Page 7) is a subsection. PE-13 (Page 10) is a section; PE-13 (1) (Page 10) is a subsection.
concerns.output_quote: Element index 27 (PE-6) is H3; Element index 31 (PE-6 (1)) is H3. Element index 40 (PE-13) is H3; Element index 42 (PE-13 (1)) is H3.
concerns.location: Sections PE-6 and PE-13, pages 6, 7, and 10.

concerns.analysis: Most lists are correctly converted with proper nesting. However, there is a structural failure at the page break between pages 11 and 12. In section PE-17, list item 'd' contains sub-items 'i' through 'vi'. Sub-item 'i' appears on page 11, while 'ii' through 'vi' appear on page 12. In the output JSON (element index 57), item 'd' only contains sub-item 'i'. Sub-items 'ii' through 'vi' are missing, resulting in an incorrectly converted and incomplete list structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PE-17 (Pages 11-12) list item 'd' contains sub-items 'i' through 'vi'.
concerns.output_quote: Element index 57 (PE-17 list) item 'd' only contains nested sub-item 'i'. Items 'ii' through 'vi' are missing.
concerns.location: PE-17 list, pages 11 and 12, element index 57.
